title: DXB Terminal 3 arrival hall struck
summary: Precision-guided munition detonated inside the Emirates check-in hall, forcing DXB to divert inbound long-haul flights to AUH and DWC.

title: DWC cargo apron cratered
summary: Follow-on drone volley damaged two pallets of US munitions; runway 12/30 closed for FOD sweep, charter lift paused 6 hrs.

title: Ras Tanura crude loading halted
summary: Anti-ship missile impact on VLCC manifold set off fire-suppression release; Aramco suspends outbound flows pending blast-door repairs.

title: US Embassy Riyadh struck by rockets
summary: Five 240mm rockets detonated along northern perimeter; chancery shelter-in-place extended, consular services offline 48 hrs.

title: Manama financial district roadblocks
summary: Youth coalitions erected burning barricades along Al Fateh Highway after 5th Fleet convoy sighted; riot police deployed LRAD.

title: US 5th Fleet pier access curtailed
summary: Base commander limited pier access to mission-essential crews while Iraqi proxy cells probed perimeter with commercial drones.

title: Strait of Hormuz declared closed to commercial traffic
summary: Joint Maritime Command laid smart mines along the inbound lane; insurers now treating all transits as constructive total loss.

title: Ballistic missile expenditure spikes
summary: Day 5 salvo fired 34 BM-25s toward UAE + KSA, doubling the first four-day average and driving projected depletion inside 9 days.

title: NEOM supply convoys diverted south
summary: Saudi logistics directorate rerouted heavy transporters via Haradh to avoid long-range fire arcs covering the coast road.

title: Duqm fuel tank farm hit
summary: Iranian Shahed-238 struck an Omani staging tank feeding coalition refuelers, trimming available jet-A uplift for UAE sorties.

title: Drone strike near US Consulate, Dubai
summary: Drone caused contained fire near US Consulate in Dubai. No injuries. Conflict day 4.

title: Two drones struck US Embassy compound, Riyadh
summary: Limited fire, minor structural damage, no casualties reported.

title: IRGC drones and missiles targeted Sheikh Isa Air Base
summary: Dawn strike on US airbase near Sheikh Isa. Bahrain activated air defenses. Details on damage withheld.

title: Drone strike on fuel tanks, Duqm commercial port (second in 48h)
summary: Multiple drones hit fuel tanks at Duqm port. Damage contained, zero casualties. Second strike on same facility within 48 hours.

title: US State Dept orders departure of non-emergency personnel from six Gulf states
summary: Non-emergency USG personnel and families ordered to depart UAE, Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Oman, Saudi Arabia.

title: QatarEnergy halts LNG production — force majeure declared
summary: Iranian strikes on Ras Laffan and Mesaieed forced complete halt of LNG production (~20% of global supply). Force majeure declared.

title: Ras Tanura refinery offline — drone debris fire
summary: Fire from intercepted drone debris shut operations at Ras Tanura refinery, Saudi Aramco's largest domestic facility.

title: Jebel Ali Port berth fire + power station complex struck
summary: Drone debris caused berth fire at Jebel Ali Port. Jebel Ali power station complex (includes desalination) also struck. ADNOC facilities under threat.

title: UAE schools closed; panic buying reported across emirates
summary: Government-ordered school closures. Supermarket and fuel station queues reported. No protests. Evacuation flights for nationals resumed on limited basis.

title: US Consulate issues shelter-in-place — imminent missile/UAV threat over Dhahran
summary: US Embassy and Consulates in Saudi Arabia issued security alert Mar 3. Shelter on lowest floor away from windows. Avoid the consulate. Saudi forces at full alert status.
source: US Embassy Saudi Arabia / TBS News

title: Aramco rerouting crude exports via Yanbu/Red Sea — Hormuz bypass activated
summary: Saudi Aramco actively rerouting crude via East-West Pipeline (Abqaiq→Yanbu, 5-7M bpd capacity). Buyers instructed to load at Yanbu to circumvent Hormuz closure. No production cuts.

title: Ras Tanura refinery shut for assessment — two drones intercepted, debris fire
summary: Two Iranian-linked drones intercepted near Ras Tanura. Debris caused contained fire. Refinery (550,000 bpd) temporarily shut. No injuries, no structural damage. Abqaiq unaffected and operational.

title: US Consulate Dubai struck by Iranian drone
summary: Iranian drone struck US Consulate Dubai — fire in parking lot and chancellery. All personnel accounted for. Significant diplomatic escalation as Iranian weapons reach US sovereign territory.

title: US Embassy Riyadh struck by 2 Iranian drones
summary: Two Iranian drones struck US Embassy compound in Riyadh. Limited fire; minor material damage. Building was relatively empty at time of strike. Diplomatic Quarter targeted.

title: QatarEnergy force majeure — ALL LNG production halted
summary: QatarEnergy declared force majeure and halted all LNG production at Ras Laffan and Mesaieed (Mar 2). Expanded to all downstream products Mar 3: urea, polymers, methanol, aluminum. 20% of global LNG supply now offline. Dutch/British wholesale gas +50-52%; Asian LNG +39%.

title: Qatar Air Force shoots down 2 Iranian SU-24 bombers
summary: Qatar Air Force intercepted and shot down 2 Iranian SU-24 bomber aircraft heading for Doha — first Gulf state to shoot down Iranian military aircraft in this conflict. Significant escalation in rules of engagement.

title: Hezbollah opens Lebanon front — rockets at Haifa, Israel authorizes ground incursion
summary: Hezbollah launched rockets and drone swarms at Ramat David airbase near Haifa — first cross-border attack since Nov 2024 ceasefire. Israel launched massive airstrikes on Beirut suburbs (52+ killed, 154 wounded) and authorized ground incursion into southern Lebanon. Conflict now multi-front.

title: Iranian drone strikes RAF Akrotiri, Cyprus — conflict reaches European territory
summary: Iranian drone struck RAF Akrotiri sovereign base area in Cyprus. Conflict has now expanded beyond the Middle East to European territory. Significant NATO implications.

title: MT Stena Imperative struck by Iranian missile at Mina Salman Port, Bahrain
summary: US oil tanker MT Stena Imperative struck by Iranian missile at Mina Salman Port, Bahrain (~02:00 UTC Mar 2). Vessel ablaze. 1 worker killed by debris. Direct strike on vessel within port perimeter.

title: US 5th Fleet HQ Juffair evacuated — NAVCENT declares area unsafe
summary: US 5th Fleet headquarters at Juffair evacuated after NAVCENT assessed area 'no longer safe for US personnel.' Radar dome took direct Shahed hit. Entire district evacuated — major loss of US naval command presence in Gulf.

title: Duqm Port struck second time — fuel storage tank hit, port suspended
summary: Duqm Port struck for second time (Mar 3). Fuel storage tank hit; no casualties. Port operations suspended. Duqm served as backup logistics hub outside Hormuz — now also offline.

title: MKD VYOM container vessel struck by drone boat 52nm from Muscat — 1 Indian killed
summary: MKD VYOM container vessel struck by kamikaze drone boat approximately 52 nautical miles from Muscat. 1 Indian crew member killed — first Indian national casualty of the conflict. Illustrates threat extends well beyond Hormuz.

title: Ras Tanura oil refinery (550k bbl/day) targeted — operations halted
summary: Ras Tanura oil refinery (550,000 bbl/day capacity) targeted by 5 drones Mar 2. Two debris impacts within perimeter; fire started. Operations halted as precaution. Brent crude $81-84/bbl (+15-20%). Analysts warn $100/bbl if disruptions persist; $120+ if sustained 3+ weeks.

title: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed — 150+ tankers at anchor, all major lines suspended
summary: IRGC declared Strait of Hormuz 'closed' Mar 2. 150+ tankers now at anchor in Gulf. All major shipping lines suspended transits: Maersk, MSC, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM, COSCO. Marine war risk insurance withdrawn effective Mar 5. GPS jamming affected 1,100+ ships in 24hrs.
